title: Here’s Something Most of the World’s Best Investors Do That You Should Be Doing Too

designed to help you do anything in your
portfolio. We don't tell you oh growth
is going up so go buy something or or or
or you know inflation is going up so go
sell something. That's not what we do.
That's what other people do. Uh what in
our opinion what we think the best
investors are the ones who put up the
most best you know the the the the the
best absolute and risk adjusted returns
and and have created the maximum amount
of wealth for their clients. Uh they by
and large use quantitative risk
management systems uh to pivot their
exposures in their portfolio because
they ultimately understand that there's
a latency uh between when you realize
something has changed from a fundamental
standpoint to when the market moves. And
you you have to use quantitative risk
management signals to uh compress that
latency. Uh and so that's what we do.
That's what a lot of other a lot of the
top funds do as well. Um we use our
global macro risk matrix to nowcast the
market regime. And the reason by the way
you reason why you want to reduce that
latency is because you want to put your
buy orders in the things that they're
going to be forced to buy depending on
the regime ahead of their buy orders so
that their flows can make you rich. You
don't want your flows to make them rich.
Conversely, you want to put your sell
orders ahead of theirs in the things
that are need to be sold when you go
from regime to regime so that their uh
flows aren't making you poor. You want
your flows to make them poor. You don't
You don't want You want their flows to
make you richer or your flows to make
them poor. You don't want it to be the
other way around. And it's You're
basically guaranteeing that it's going
to be the other way around into the
negative way uh if you are using you
know you're overly reliant on
fundamental research to make changes in
your portfolio. You have to have a high
quality
repeatable quantitative risk management
overlay if you want to stay you know at
the vanguard of these changes and these
big pivots and flows across the global
buy side. And so that's why we our our
process is organized like this. I know
for a fact that you know many of the top
funds across the global buy side risk
process risk management process is
organized like this. So you know we'll
keep it moving here. Uh we use our
global macro risk matrix to nowcast the
market regime which investors must
position for to avoid FOMO or FOMO. Fear
of missing out causes investors to buy
cycle tops and fear of more losses
causes investors to sell cycle lows. The
two worst things you can do as an
investor. So you know you know FOMO
You You only experience FOMO and FOMO
when you're you've already committed a
type two error. FOMO I I didn't buy a
developing bull market because I'm so
afraid of of of another uh you know
false alarm. And so now as a developing
bull market has run away from my
portfolio now I have FOMO and now I got
to capitulate and buy high and that's
when I buy the cycle top. Or if I have
FOMO I didn't respect the developing
bear market because I'm so afraid of a
false alarm and eventually this
developing bear market has caused me to
lose so much money that I have fear of
more losses so I find up selling cycle
low to to to to to cut the to cut the
stress to lower the stress levels.
Those are the two worst things you can
do as an investor. You know you type two
errors will will invariably lead to FOMO
and FOMO and FOMO and FOMO will
invariably lead to you doing the worst
possible thing you can do as an
investor. So you have to make a choice.
I say this every week.
You have to make a choice
as an investor. And if you don't make
the choice you're guaranteed to be in
the wrong category. If you want to break
up you know investing returns into the
four different quantiles you know the
type one errors live here you know you
know kind of in the small smallish to
medium you know loss category and type
two errors uh live here. I I did that
very poorly. It's not one. It looks like
There we go. Type one type two here.
You don't want to be here. You don't
want to be here.
Being here is when you blow up your
portfolio. Being here is when you have a
destabilizing drawdown or you fail to
maximize upside capture to such a degree
that you don't move towards a long time
and comfortable retirement. It's worse
works in both directions not just
markets going down. It's also markets
going up. You have to respect developing
bull markets. You have to respect
developing bear markets. Not every time
KISS signals is a is are we going to
have a sustained you know low volatility
uptrend if we go from a RoRo phase
transition from a risk off market regime
to a risk on market regime and not every
time KISS has a
RoRo phase transition from a risk on
market regime to a risk off market
regime are we going to have a secular
bear market. That's not the that's not
the goal. The goal is to dispassionate
respond to change to signals that give
you the highest probability
of remaining on the right side of market
risk. The you know the it could be a
short correction. Could be a
developing secular bear market. You
don't know in real time. None of us
knows where the market's going. A lot of
people think they know but it's usually
a function of the Dunning-Kruger effect
and people's lack of you know lack of
insight and lack of wisdom and lack of
analytical ability and lack of
forecasting ability that gives them the
confidence to say I know where markets
are headed. The more you study the more
you know the more data you analyze the
more humility you develop in saying oh I
think I actually don't know. I'm going
to trust the wisdom of the crowd here
which is what our global macro risk
matrix does. It helps us uh do.
